posts.timeText: 1. Been cruising around on a artpro 1201 and png 1300v2 for a while. Feel like I’ve had enough sessions to have a break through, but jibes just aren’t getting consistent. The turns are good but the foot swaps are launching or diving. I can ride twisted indefinitely, or goofy. I was using a skinny 360/45 for a while, but I had a 380 speed lying around and figured since it was bigger it would help.. I don’t notice a difference. (I was landing jibes 90% on giant gofoil 2200 and 17” stab…probably not saying much haha)

·
Hey gang,
2 questions! Sorry, it’s long— always starts short and gets long when you’re describing this stuff.
1. Been cruising around on a artpro 1201 and png 1300v2 for a while. Feel like I’ve had enough sessions to have a break through, but jibes just aren’t getting consistent. The turns are good but the foot swaps are launching or diving. I can ride twisted indefinitely, or goofy. I was using a skinny 360/45 for a while, but I had a 380 speed lying around and figured since it was bigger it would help.. I don’t notice a difference. (I was landing jibes 90% on giant gofoil 2200 and 17” stab…probably not saying much haha)
2. I moved the mast to “center weighted” using the upside down balance, then micro adjusted for nice stable/level flight, but I always feel like I’m riding slightly down hill or having to add too much back foot pressure, should I step back more? I might end up on or behind the mast?! I I I have regular mast tracks, I was thinking of putting in an advance fuse for turning but nervous about pitch stability changes and having mast-forward. Maybe a regular advance not + for now?
More practice or a lesson? — maybe a little gear adjustment could help? Progressive stab?
75kg
Standard ultrashort fuse
5-6m wing (more success on 5m in general)
F ONE 7’ downwinder
10-16kn wind range.
Thank you for the tips, as always,
Eric
7 comments
Like
Comment
Send
Most relevant
FoilBeat
On 1 AP1201:
For winging use longer fuse and bigger stab. Personal favorite is Advance+ Short with Progressive 375. Advance+ is the new standard, but your tracks must allow to move your mast forward by a couple inches.
On 1 PNG1300v2:
Same thing. You might consider an even bigger stab like Progressive 425 to make it a balanced setup.
On 2:
In general, stand as comfortable as possible no matter where your foil is mounted. A small stab may make the plane a bit tail heavy. Things might improve once you solve 1. Finally, I don’t know about F-One but foil angles are not standardized between brands. If the above doesn’t solve the problem you might think about shimming the mast.
8h
Like
Reply
1
Reply to FoilBeat…
kiwi1385
I had the same issues , came from duotone,bathtub board could gybe foot switch . Wanted more dw glide . Brought 1300 v1 , spitfire 1030 , dw 107 board , 600 fuse , couldn't gybe couldn't tack switch foot . It was like starting all over again .
Then I got a hps1050 standard short fuse 700 ,prog 400 stab and it all start to click again.
We have light winds 8 to 14 knots , I'm 80 kg love up wind dw wing .
2 yrs on I love my spitfires 1180 and 1030 . Playing in bumps , longer fuse for going up wind . 350 prog . Now I want the next foil buzz , more glide , a litte faster , to keep up with swells bumps ??? Big foils are tricky in bumps , so affect by turbalance.
1d
Like
Reply
1
Fares Lahouel
Stick with one setup for a while get adjusted to it give it some time. No matter what you choose after a while you will get comfortable with it. Then move on.
1d
Like
Reply
1
Charles Bessard
I have heard that some of the f-one board had some tail rocker and were requiring to shim the mast. Maybe this is what you need to check. You may need a positive rake shim on your base plate?
1d
Like
Reply
1
Ray Quahs
I'm on a 980 HPS and had that same downhill riding experience, put a shim (credit card) in the stabiliser back end to make the front turn up more, feels better now. I'm just getting a few jibes in, but the SF 960 was giving me a hard time.
1d
Like
Reply
Eric Dornbush
Yeah, I really don’t have a hard time turning it. It’s the pop n switch of the jibe that’s the problem. I’m used to big carvy-ness of longboard (skate and surf) and been using big span foils for a while so I’m pretty good at committing to the turn and pulling out of it.
With the skinny I had a blast in a wing-upwind, ride downwind where I didn’t need to foot switch,
1d
Like
Reply
Perry Hughes
I have a 1300 V2 and these big span wings are harder to turn than your average foil. Grab a cheap SF 960 or similar and you will be amazed. Skinny 45 works great with the 1300v2 btw.
1d
Like
Reply
